name: Buttermilk Fried Chicken with Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the chicken in a large shallow dish. Pour buttermilk over; cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.
2. Combine flour, salt, and pepper in a double-strength paper bag.
3. Drain the chicken pieces; toss one at a time in the flour mixture. Shake off excess; place on waxed paper for 15 minutes to dry.
4. Heat oil (1/8-1/4 inch) in a large skillet. Fry chicken until browned on all sides. Cover and simmer, turning occasionally, for 40-45 minutes, or until juices run clear and chicken is tender. Uncover and cook an additional 5 minutes.
5. Remove the chicken and keep warm. Drain all but 1/4 cup of drippings in the skillet.
6. Stir in flour until bubbly. Add milk and water; cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Cook for 1 minute more. Add remaining water if needed. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Serve gravy over chicken.
